add.title: Missing POST /api/v1/cart/items endpoint specification
add.description: Story requires 'POST /api/v1/cart/items (Add product, default quantity: 1)' but LLD only documents GET, PUT, and DELETE endpoints. The add-to-cart functionality is missing complete API specification.
add.recommended_action: Add complete POST /api/v1/cart/items endpoint specification including request/response schemas, default quantity handling, and error scenarios

add.title: Missing automatic recalculation logic specification
add.description: Story explicitly requires 'Automatic recalculation of subtotal and total upon any quantity mutation' but LLD does not document the trigger mechanism, calculation sequence, or real-time update behavior.
add.recommended_action: Add detailed specification for automatic recalculation triggers, calculation order (item subtotal -> cart total), and state management approach

add.title: Missing empty cart redirection specification
add.description: Story requires 'Empty cart state must trigger specific UI redirection (continue shopping link)' but LLD does not specify empty cart handling, UI state, or redirection behavior.
add.recommended_action: Add empty cart state handling specification including UI component behavior, redirection target, and user messaging

add.title: Missing Price Snapshot field in CartItem entity
add.description: Story specifies CartItem must contain 'current Price Snapshot' but LLD CartItem entity only includes productId, quantity, and addedAt. Price snapshot is critical for order integrity.
add.recommended_action: Add priceSnapshot field to CartItem entity with data type (Decimal), precision requirements, and snapshot capture timing specification

add.title: Missing Quantity Updater Component specification
add.description: Story requires 'Quantity Updater Component' in presentation layer but LLD does not document this interactive component, its validation rules, or update mechanism.
add.recommended_action: Add Quantity Updater Component specification including input validation (min/max), increment/decrement controls, and API integration pattern

add.title: Missing Payment Processing Module integration touchpoint
add.description: Epic identifies 'Payment Processing Module' as impacted system component and cart management must prepare data for checkout/payment flow, but LLD does not document integration points or data handoff.
add.recommended_action: Add specification for cart-to-checkout data transition, including cart validation requirements before payment initiation

add.title: Missing Notification Service integration for cart events
add.description: Epic includes 'Notification & Email Service' as impacted component. Cart abandonment, price changes, or stock updates may require notifications, but LLD has no notification integration.
add.recommended_action: Evaluate and document if cart management requires notification triggers (e.g., cart abandonment reminders, price drop alerts)

add.title: Missing test case alignment with Story QA tasks
add.description: Story defines specific QA tasks (SCRUM-1207, 1209, 1211, 1212) for backend unit tests, frontend unit tests, functional tests, and automation scripts, but LLD does not reference testability requirements or test data specifications.
add.recommended_action: Add testability section documenting test data requirements, mock service needs, and validation criteria for each QA task type

modify.title: GET /api/v1/cart endpoint incomplete - missing individual subtotals
modify.description: Story requires 'GET /api/v1/cart (Retrieve items, individual subtotals, and cart total)' but LLD response schema only shows items array and total. Individual item subtotals are missing from response structure.
modify.current_state: LLD documents GET /api/v1/cart with response containing items[] and total only
modify.required_state: Response must include subtotal for each CartItem (quantity × priceSnapshot)
modify.source: Story Summary - api_contracts_required vs LLD Section 5.2
modify.recommended_action: Modify GET /api/v1/cart response schema to include 'subtotal' field in each CartItem object, calculated as quantity × priceSnapshot

modify.title: ShoppingCart entity missing session/user binding specification
modify.description: Story specifies 'ShoppingCart (Entity bound to user session or ID)' but LLD ShoppingCart entity only shows userId field without clarifying session-based vs authenticated user binding strategy.
modify.current_state: LLD ShoppingCart has userId (String) without session binding specification
modify.required_state: Clear specification of session-based (anonymous) vs user-based (authenticated) cart binding, including session-to-user migration on login
modify.source: Story Summary - data_model_impact vs LLD Section 4.1
modify.recommended_action: Modify ShoppingCart entity documentation to specify dual-mode binding: sessionId for anonymous users, userId for authenticated users, and cart merge logic on authentication

modify.title: Cart total calculation logic incomplete
modify.description: LLD mentions 'total is sum of all item prices' but Story requires subtotal-per-item and cart-level total with automatic recalculation. Current specification lacks calculation granularity and trigger specification.
modify.current_state: LLD states total calculation without subtotal breakdown or recalculation triggers
modify.required_state: Detailed calculation flow: item.subtotal = quantity × priceSnapshot, cart.total = Σ(item.subtotal), with triggers on quantity update, item add/remove
modify.source: Story Summary - business_logic_rules vs LLD Section 3
modify.recommended_action: Expand business logic section to document two-tier calculation (item subtotal, cart total), calculation triggers, and state update sequence

modify.title: PUT /api/v1/cart/items/{itemId} missing quantity validation rules
modify.description: Story implies quantity modification with validation (Quantity Updater Component with min/max) but LLD PUT endpoint does not specify quantity constraints, validation rules, or error responses for invalid quantities.
modify.current_state: LLD PUT endpoint shows quantity update without validation specification
modify.required_state: Explicit quantity validation rules (min: 1, max: stock availability or business limit), error codes for validation failures
modify.source: Story Summary - presentation_layer_needs (Quantity Updater Component) vs LLD Section 5.2
modify.recommended_action: Modify PUT /api/v1/cart/items/{itemId} specification to include quantity validation rules, min/max constraints, and 400 error responses for invalid quantities

modify.title: Cart View Interface specification incomplete
modify.description: Story requires 'Cart View Interface' and 'Empty Cart View with Redirection Link' but LLD only mentions 'Cart Management Page' without distinguishing populated vs empty states or specifying redirection behavior.
modify.current_state: LLD mentions Cart Management Page without state-specific UI specifications
modify.required_state: Separate specifications for populated cart view (item list, quantity controls, totals) and empty cart view (messaging, 'continue shopping' link with target)
modify.source: Story Summary - presentation_layer_needs vs LLD Section 6
modify.recommended_action: Modify UI Components section to document two cart view states: populated (with item management controls) and empty (with redirection to product catalog/homepage)

modify.title: Component interaction with Product Catalog Service underspecified
modify.description: Epic identifies 'Product Catalog / Search Service' as impacted component. LLD mentions product data retrieval but does not specify real-time price validation, stock availability checks, or product existence verification during cart operations.
modify.current_state: LLD shows basic product data retrieval without validation workflows
modify.required_state: Specification of Product Catalog Service integration for: price validation on cart load, product existence check on add, stock availability verification
modify.source: Epic Summary - system_components_impacted vs LLD Section 3
modify.recommended_action: Modify architecture section to document Product Catalog Service integration points: price sync mechanism, product validation on add-to-cart, and stock check before checkout

delete.title: Wishlist/Save for Later functionality not in Story scope
delete.description: LLD Section 3 mentions 'Users can save items for later (wishlist functionality)' but this is not present in Story SCRUM-1140 requirements or Epic SCRUM-1138 scope. This represents scope creep.
delete.current_state: LLD documents wishlist/save-for-later as part of cart management
delete.justification: Story SCRUM-1140 scope is limited to: add to cart, view cart, modify quantities, remove items. Wishlist is a separate feature not included in Epic child stories roadmap.
delete.source: LLD Section 3 vs Story Summary scope
delete.traceability: No corresponding Story or Epic requirement
delete.recommended_action: Remove wishlist/save-for-later functionality from LLD or create separate Story for this feature if business requires it

delete.title: Guest checkout mention without Epic/Story support
delete.description: LLD mentions 'guest users can add items to cart' but Epic SCRUM-1138 and Story SCRUM-1140 do not specify guest vs authenticated user handling. This creates ambiguity about authentication requirements.
delete.current_state: LLD implies guest checkout capability without specification
delete.justification: Neither Epic nor Story define guest user workflows, authentication requirements, or session-to-user cart migration. This should be clarified or removed to avoid implementation ambiguity.
delete.source: LLD Section 3 vs Epic/Story authentication requirements (not specified)
delete.traceability: No explicit Epic/Story requirement for guest user handling
delete.recommended_action: Remove guest user references or escalate to product owner for explicit guest checkout Story creation and authentication strategy definition

delete.title: Redundant 'addedAt' timestamp without business use case
delete.description: LLD CartItem entity includes 'addedAt' timestamp field, but neither Epic nor Story define time-based cart features (expiration, sorting by add time, analytics). This field has no traced requirement.
delete.justification: No Epic/Story requirement for cart item timestamps. Story only requires: product reference, quantity, price snapshot. Additional fields should have explicit requirements.
delete.traceability: No corresponding Epic/Story requirement for timestamp tracking
delete.recommended_action: Remove addedAt field unless there is an undocumented requirement for cart expiration, item age tracking, or analytics (requires Story clarification)
